_disclaimer: DEMO CONTENT — synthetic data for local development and demos only. Not derived from real assessments.
content:
title: Kubernetes Security Baseline to FedRAMP Mapping
metadata:
  type: MappingDocument
  id: ksb-fedramp
  gemara-version: 1.0.0
  version: 1.0.0
  description: Maps Kubernetes security baseline controls to FedRAMP Moderate AC and SC families
  author:
    id: complytime
    name: ComplyTime
    type: Software Assisted
  mapping-references:
    - id: kube-security-baseline
      title: Kubernetes Security Baseline Controls
      version: 1.0.0
    - id: fedramp-moderate
      title: FedRAMP Moderate Baseline
      version: Rev5
source-reference:
  entry-type: Control
  reference-id: kube-security-baseline
target-reference:
  entry-type: AssessmentRequirement
  reference-id: fedramp-moderate
mappings:
  - id: ksb1-to-fedramp
    source: KSB-1
    relationship: supports
    targets:
      - entry-id: AC-6
        strength: 8
        confidence-level: High
        rationale: >-
          Pod security standards enforce least privilege at the workload level,
          directly supporting AC-6 Least Privilege.
      - entry-id: SC-7
        strength: 6
        confidence-level: Medium
        rationale: >-
          Pod security constraints contribute to boundary protection by
          restricting workload capabilities.
  - id: ksb2-to-fedramp
    source: KSB-2
    relationship: supports
    targets:
      - entry-id: SC-7
        strength: 9
        confidence-level: High
        rationale: >-
          Network policies implement boundary protection within the cluster,
          directly supporting SC-7.
  - id: ksb3-to-fedramp
    source: KSB-3
    relationship: supports
    targets:
      - entry-id: SC-28
        strength: 7
        confidence-level: Medium
        rationale: >-
          Image signing provides protection of information at rest (image integrity)
          supporting SC-28.
      - entry-id: AC-3
        strength: 6
        confidence-level: Medium
        rationale: >-
          Image admission control enforces access to run only authorized images.
  - id: ksb4-to-fedramp
    source: KSB-4
    relationship: supports
    targets:
      - entry-id: AC-6
        strength: 9
        confidence-level: High
        rationale: >-
          RBAC least-privilege directly implements AC-6 Least Privilege.
      - entry-id: AC-3
        strength: 8
        confidence-level: High
        rationale: >-
          RBAC enforcement directly implements AC-3 Access Enforcement.
  - id: ksb5-to-fedramp
    source: KSB-5
    relationship: supports
    targets:
      - entry-id: SC-28
        strength: 9
        confidence-level: High
        rationale: >-
          Secrets encryption at rest directly implements SC-28 Protection
          of Information at Rest.
